phase: Return to Base Building + Intensity Introduction
weekly_summary: CRITICAL RESET WEEK: Athlete is undertrained (< 10 km/wk vs. 50+ km/wk capacity for marathon prep). This week introduces structured 4-run template with first quality session (tempo work). No long run yet — athlete must rebuild frequency and consistency before volume surge. Focus: adherence to schedule, re-establishing aerobic rhythm, light intensity probe.
coaching_notes: This athlete is in DETRAINING CRISIS — 8 weeks of declining volume with 1–2 runs/week is incompatible with marathon goal. Weeks 2–8 show erratic, sparse activity (likely life disruption: work, illness, injury, or motivation loss). IMMEDIATE ACTION: Establish rock-solid 4-run/week routine (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun easy, Thu quality). Current paces (5:47–6:18 min/km) are sustainable but indicate NO threshold training. Introduce tempo Thursday to rebuild VO2 max and lactate clearance. Do NOT attempt long runs (15+ km) until 4-week consistency proven — athlete lacks base. No HR data is a major blind spot; acquisition of HR monitor is prerequisite for load management. If marathon is < 12 weeks away, recommend deferral to 2027. If 16+ weeks, this plan restores trajectory but requires iron discipline on the 4-run minimum.
recovery_flags: High cumulative fatigue from 8-week undertraining — athlete may feel sluggish early week
Re-entry soreness likely despite low recent volume — expect DOMS on Wednesday/Thursday
Sleep/nutrition may have degraded during sparse-run period — prioritize sleep 8–9 hrs/night
Mental fatigue from motivation loss — use short, achievable runs to rebuild confidence
next_week_preview: Week 10 (Jun 29–Jul 5): If adherence to 4-run template is 100%, progress to 36 km with slightly longer easy runs (8–9 km Mon/Wed/Fri) and sustained 25 min tempo Thursday. Introduce Sunday long run at 11 km (first true long-run session since Week 2). Do NOT attempt 15+ km until Week 11. Monitor for signs of overuse; current baseline is low so ramp rate is cautious.

days.title: Easy Base — Consistency Builder
days.description: Relaxed 8 km at steady easy pace (~6:00 min/km). Focus on nasal breathing, rhythm, form. Zero speed work — mental reset after sparse weeks. Terrain: flat preferred. Expect to feel slightly sluggish first 2 km; this normalizes by km 3.
days.notes: First of 4-run weekly commitment. Non-negotiable anchor day. If any pain/niggles, WALK final 1 km rather than push through.

days.title: Active Recovery / Mobility
days.description: Full rest day. Athlete may do light 15–20 min walk if desired, but no running. Use for: sleep (8+ hrs), nutrition (carbs + protein post-run Mon), foam rolling (calves, quads, IT band), dynamic stretching. Hydration focus. Prepare mentally for Thursday tempo work.
days.notes: Sleep quality is critical — poor sleep undermines entire week. Aim for bed by 10:30 pm.

days.title: Easy Base — Mid-Week Aerobic Top-up
days.description: Repeat Monday's 8 km easy run. Same pace (~6:00 min/km), flat terrain. This is second anchor run — consistency > pace. Legs should feel fresher than Monday. If very tired, reduce to 6.5 km rather than skip. Do NOT do threshold/tempo work today (Thursday is reserved for quality).
days.notes: Check avg pace trending toward 5:55–5:50 min/km by end of run — should feel smoother than Monday.

days.title: Threshold Development — First Intensity Session
days.description: STRUCTURE: 10 min easy warm-up (Z2, ~6:10 min/km) → 20 min tempo main set (Z3, ~5:30 min/km, 'comfortably hard' effort) → 10 min easy cool-down (Z2, ~6:10 min/km). Total 10 km. This is first VO2 max / lactate threshold stimulus in 8 weeks. Athlete should feel breathing elevated during 20 min block but able to speak short sentences. If impossible to sustain, reduce tempo block to 15 min and extend warm-up to 12 min.
days.key_focus: Aerobic capacity, lactate clearance, VO2 max stimulus
days.notes: DO NOT attempt all-out pace. 'Comfortably hard' = Z3, not Z4/Z5. Heart rate will feel high but should settle into steady rhythm by min 5 of tempo block. This is PROBE week — establishing baseline for future tempo progressions.

days.title: Recovery + Preparation
days.description: Full rest. Muscles are recovering from Thursday tempo. Do light yoga (20 min), mobility work, or walk if desired. Hydration + carbs + protein critical. Sleep target 8+ hrs (prepare for Sunday long run stimulus). Avoid alcohol, late meals.
days.notes: Friday rest is non-negotiable for 4-run template — prevents overuse injury and allows Friday night sleep quality.

days.title: Easy Base — Pre-Long-Run Primer
days.description: Short, easy 6 km at ~6:00 min/km. Legs should feel rested after Friday rest. This run 'primes' muscles for Sunday's first true long-run session in 8 weeks. Keep it relaxed — no pace pushing. Flat terrain preferred. Use as mental rehearsal for Sunday rhythm.
days.notes: If fatigue carries over from Thursday, shorten to 5 km. Quality of Sunday run > Saturday distance.

days.title: Long Run Reintroduction (Aerobic Base)
days.description: FIRST STRUCTURED LONG RUN in 8 weeks. Conservative 10 km at easy, conversational pace (~6:00 min/km). This is VOLUME stimulus, not pace stimulus — entire run should feel sustainable (able to hold full sentences). Start slow (first 2 km ~6:15), settle into rhythm, finish strong if legs feel good. Athlete should walk final 500 m if needed (no shame). Fuel strategy: light breakfast 2 hrs before (toast + banana), water every 2 km, light carb snack within 30 min post-run.
days.key_focus: Aerobic endurance, mental toughness, long-run routine
days.notes: This is foundation week — 10 km is conservative but establishes consistency. Next week: 11 km. Target: reach 20–22 km by Week 16–18. Do NOT skip this run; it is core marathon prep stimulus.
